title: niphogeton azorelloides
intro: Niphogeton azorelloides, a plant species belonging to the family Araliaceae, is native to the Galapagos Islands and is also known as Galapagos angelica or Galapagos snakeroot. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a potential medicinal plant. Niphogeton azorelloides has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms associated with these conditions, providing potential relief to those suffering from them.
